### Post by jsdill on 2007-06-08
Folks...anyone see what is wrong with this? It is not keeping the bad guys out. I've removed the actual ips:

# Generated by iptables-save v1.3.3 on Fri Jun  8 06:37:43 2007
*filter
:INPUT ACCEPT [2771363:804285333]
:FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [3160269:1348786919]
-A INPUT -s ip_address -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable
-A INPUT -s ip_address -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable
-A INPUT -s ip_address -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable
-A INPUT -s ip_address -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable
-A INPUT -s ip_address -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-port-unreachable
COMMIT
# Completed on Fri Jun  8 06:37:43 2007

...Jordan

### Post by Zzl1xndd on 2007-06-08
It looks like the connections from those Ip's are being rejected thus it is doing as it should, although this is probably just what is refered to as Background Noise. What you might wanna do if you are worried is install Firestarter to give you a little more grapical conrtol over the firewall and maybe just check to see if your coming up as Stealth with Sheilds up and [www.GRC.com](www.GRC.com)

### Post by ikonia on 2007-06-08
what do you mean not keeping the bad guys out ?

what are you seeing thats worrying you.

### Post by jsdill on 2007-06-08
> **ikonia said:**
> what do you mean not keeping the bad guys out ?

what are you seeing thats worrying you.

Hi...multiple attempts to log in despite a particular ip being set up to be rejected. My understanding is that 
once an ip is listed that should close the door.

...Jordan

### Post by Zzl1xndd on 2007-06-08
> **jsdill said:**
> Hi...multiple attempts to log in despite a particular ip being set up to be rejected. My understanding is that 
once an ip is listed that should close the door.

...Jordan

it does keep them from logging in but not from trying. Think of it like a door just because its locked doesn't mean people can't knock.

---

### Post by ikonia on 2007-06-08
how are they trying to login ? ssh I assume. If so set your sshd daemon to use tcp wrapper and deny the ip address.
